title: Willughbeia lanceolata
intro: Willughbeia lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the lance-leaf Willughbeia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Willughbeia lanceolata include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or processed into a tincture or dried extract, which are used to treat various health conditions.
